observations: SCOPE OF THIS PASS: a full round-3 re-review (not a metadata-only delta), since revision 3 adds four new operative verification entries (5, 8, 9, 14) closing Validator Mode A's round-2 rejection (three BLOCKER/MAJOR findings) plus this evaluator's own rev2 non-blocking anchor residual, folded in at zero extra revision cost. This is the orchestrator-declared FINAL permitted revision round; all four kill-shots were independently reproduced from scratch in both directions, not trusted from the generator's or Mode A's own narrative.

SCHEMA VALIDITY AND ARRAY LENGTH: `jsonschema.validate` re-run against the live revision-3 contract and schema/contract.schema.json: exits 0, schema-valid. Parsed the live 'verification' array programmatically: exactly 35 entries, matching evidence_convention's own stated count (31 -> 35, +4 new entries). No off-by-one. Programmatic sweep of all 35 commands found zero ungated 'echo PASS' occurrences (every 'echo PASS' is preceded by '&&' or an 'if/then' gate).

KILL-SHOT 1 RE-VERIFICATION (new entry 5, status enum, closes Mode A round-2's BLOCKER): reran entry 5's exact command against the STRICT scratch schema -- PASSES ('reopened' and 'closed' rejected; 'open' and 'resolved'-with-closure_evidence both validate). Built the exact GAP variant Mode A's rejection describes (status relaxed to a bare untyped string, no enum): reran entry 5's identical command against it -- FAILS ('status=reopened unexpectedly validated'). Additionally reproduced the SPECIFIC combined kill-shot Mode A's finding calls out by name: under the GAP variant, an instance with status='closed' and NO closure_evidence key at all VALIDATES (confirmed empirically: 'GAP (no status enum): status="closed" + NO closure_evidence -- WRONGLY VALIDATES'), proving the closure_evidence conditional's own const:'resolved' guard has nothing to say about an out-of-enum status value unless the enum itself forecloses it first -- exactly the mechanism the finding describes, now closed under STRICT.

KILL-SHOT 2 RE-VERIFICATION (new entry 8, path-pattern anchor, closes this evaluator's own rev2 non-blocking residual): reran entry 8's exact command against STRICT -- PASSES (a nested path 'foo/.agent/evidence/bar.txt', containing the substring but not as a true prefix, is rejected). Built the exact UN-ANCHORED GAP variant from this evaluator's own rev2 review (pattern relaxed from '^\\.agent/evidence/' to bare '\\.agent/evidence/', anchor dropped): reran entry 8's identical command against it -- FAILS ('nested path unexpectedly validated'), reproducing this evaluator's own prior finding precisely. The rev2 residual is now genuinely closed, not merely noted.

KILL-SHOT 3 RE-VERIFICATION (new entry 9, top-level evidence minItems, closes Mode A round-2's MAJOR): reran entry 9's exact command against STRICT -- PASSES (evidence:[] rejected). Built the exact GAP variant (top-level 'evidence' property's minItems:1 dropped, item-shape and $ref retained): reran entry 9's identical command against it -- FAILS ('an empty top-level evidence array ([]) was NOT rejected'), confirming the fix is genuinely load-bearing and distinct from entry 6's missing-path/missing-revision item-shape probes (which only ever exercise item shape when at least one item is present).

KILL-SHOT 4 RE-VERIFICATION (new entry 14, closure_evidence minItems under resolved, closes Mode A round-2's MAJOR): reran entry 14's exact command against STRICT -- PASSES (resolved + closure_evidence:[] rejected). Built the exact GAP variant (the allOf/then clause's own re-assertion of closure_evidence relaxed to drop minItems:1, item-shape/$ref retained): reran entry 14's identical command against it -- FAILS ('resolved finding with an empty closure_evidence array ([]) was NOT rejected'), confirming the fix is genuinely distinct from both the missing-key case (entry 12's sibling probes / the named negative fixture) and the present-but-structurally-empty-item case (closure_evidence:[{}], entry 13).

ALL FOUR ROUND-2 FINDINGS (three from Mode A, one from this evaluator's own rev2 non-blocking note) ARE THEREFORE GENUINELY CLOSED, PROVEN IN BOTH DIRECTIONS (GAP variant fails the new entry; STRICT/actual design passes it), not merely asserted closed.

SOUNDNESS CARRIED FORWARD, INDEPENDENTLY RE-PROBED (not assumed from prior rounds): re-ran all 18 content-anchored, non-git-dependent entries (1, 3-19) against the disposable STRICT minitree -- all 18 PASS. Re-ran the LOOSE closure-conditional variant (allOf deleted, closure_evidence relaxed to untyped array) against entries 12, 13, 16, 18, 19 (renumbered from rev2's 9, 10, 12, 13/14, 15): entries 13 and 18 correctly FAIL under LOOSE (content-completeness [{}] probe and the named resolved-without-closure-evidence fixture check), entries 12, 16, 19 pass under LOOSE too but that is expected -- they prove narrower, different properties, not the conditional's strictness. The 8-required-key omission loop (entry 10, renumbered from rev2's entry 7) independently re-run against STRICT: still PASSES, all 8 keys still load-bearing for absence. Entry 11 (minLength on 5 scalar fields, renumbered from rev2's entry 8) and entry 15 (maturity exact-vocabulary, renumbered from rev2's entry 11) and entry 7 (original wrong-prefix path-pattern probe, renumbered from rev2's entry 6) all independently re-run against STRICT: all PASS.

DUAL-SURFACE NIZAM.json PAIR RE-VERIFIED AGAINST THE CURRENT REAL NIZAM.json (entries 22+23, renumbered from rev2's 18+19; NIZAM.json itself is unchanged since revision 2, still at post-8c7c3ee state): built three fresh disposable scratch NIZAM.json variants from the CURRENT, live NIZAM.json -- top-level-'schemas'-only, module-'key_documents'-only, and fully dual-compliant. Ran entries 22 and 23's exact, live command bodies against all three: top-level-only -> entry22 FAILS / entry23 PASSES; module-only -> entry22 PASSES / entry23 FAILS; dual-compliant -> both PASS. The pair still fully and uniquely distinguishes all three cases; the round-0 gap remains closed.

FINGERPRINT AT HEAD 8c7c3ee RE-CONFIRMED LIVE, READ-ONLY, ACROSS ALL GIT/NIZAM/README-DEPENDENT ENTRIES (2, 20-35): results exactly match the expected pre-implementation state, unchanged in substance from the rev2 review (shifted only by the four new entries' insertion positions): entries 2, 21, 25, 27, 28, 29, 30, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35 PASS; entries 20, 22, 23, 24, 26 correctly FAIL (schema/README.md and NIZAM.json have not yet been edited by an unimplemented 039; the working-tree schema/README.md version, 0.6.0, is not yet a strict increase over HEAD's own 0.6.0 -- exactly the design_notes-predicted pre-implementation state, unchanged since revision 2 since HEAD has not moved and 039 has still not implemented). The guard/scope-guard simplifications from revision 2 (dropping now-unneeded tolerances for 036's/037's since-committed paths) were not re-derived a third time since nothing about the real repository's tracked/committed state changed between the rev2 and rev3 reviews; their live-PASS re-confirmation in this pass is sufficient corroboration.

SEVERITY-NOT-AN-ENUM AND CONFIDENCE-ENUM DESIGN DECISIONS: design_notes.severity_and_status_scope and the relevant parts of schema_design are byte-identical to revisions 1 and 2 (no textual change in revision 3 beyond the four new verification entries and their approvals-block bookkeeping); this evaluator's prior verbatim confirmation against ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1 and Sec 5 therefore still holds and was not re-derived from scratch a third time, consistent with house convention for unchanged claims already independently verified twice.

FRESH-DEFECT SWEEP ON THE FOUR NEW ENTRIES: entry 5's status-enum probe correctly excludes 'status' from its own 'base' dict (mirroring entry 4's confidence-enum pattern) so the per-branch assertion signal is attributable to the enum alone, not a masked, unrelated missing-field failure; confirmed by inspection that neither bad1 ('reopened') nor bad2 ('closed') sets status to 'resolved', so the closure_evidence allOf/then conditional's own const-gate never fires for either probe -- any rejection is attributable solely to the enum, not cross-contaminated by the conditional (directly confirmed by the GAP-variant run: with the enum dropped, 'reopened' validates immediately with no closure_evidence present, proving the enum was the sole guard under STRICT). Entry 8's nested-path probe and entry 9's/14's minItems probes were each traced by hand for early-exit/exit-code correctness (sys.exit/SystemExit-inside-try patterns, matching the already-vetted structure of entries 7 (formerly rev2's 6) and 13); no ungated echo, no shared-mutable-state leakage across probes, and no cross-branch masking found in any of the four.

ONE PRE-EXISTING, NON-BLOCKING OBSERVATION CARRIED FORWARD FOR THE RECORD (unchanged design text since revision 0, not newly introduced or newly discovered in this pass, and not gating approval at this final round): schema_design's prose describes 'id' as 'unique within the audit run', but no verification entry (in any of the four revisions) tests cross-record id uniqueness. This is judged non-blocking because it is structurally out of this schema's own reach: schema/engineering_finding.schema.json validates a SINGLE finding object, not the findings.json array that would hold multiple findings and could therefore express a uniqueness constraint (that artifact-level schema, if any, is downstream CLI/assembly scope per this contract's own non-goals, e.g. feature 041). This has been present, unchanged, and implicitly accepted across all three prior approvals (validator round0/round2, evaluator round1/round2); it is recorded here only for completeness, not as a new finding.

CONCURRENCY / PROBE-ISOLATION: confirmed live throughout this review that HEAD remains 8c7c3ee (feature 037 fully committed, unchanged since the rev2 review), feature 039 remains 'proposed' with its own untracked .agent/contracts/039.json, and feature 040's untracked .agent/contracts/040.json (drafting) was tolerated and never opened or touched, per the probe-isolation instruction. `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this review's probes shows only 039's own untracked contract/review artifacts (including this review's own new file and the prior rev1/rev2 review artifacts), 040's own untracked contract, and the pre-existing v0.6.0-release-notes.md; `git diff HEAD --stat` shows no tracked-file changes. All scratch schemas, fixtures, and disposable NIZAM.json variants used in this round-3 review live only under the session scratchpad directory, entirely outside the repository tree. Nothing under schema/, ecosystem/, tools/, docs/, or NIZAM.json in the real repository, and no other feature's .agent/contracts or .agent/qa artifact, was created, edited, or deleted at any point in this review. No edit was made to .agent/run_state.json.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 3, fit for implementation as-is. All four gaps closed since the last approved state (three from Validator Mode A's round-2 rejection -- status-enum negative probe, top-level evidence:[] boundary, resolved+closure_evidence:[] boundary -- plus this evaluator's own rev2 non-blocking path-pattern-anchor residual, folded in at zero extra revision cost) are independently reproduced as live kill-shots in both directions: each new entry (5, 8, 9, 14) PASSES against the contract's real, unmodified STRICT schema_design and correctly FAILS against a disposable scratch GAP variant reproducing the exact defect class each finding describes (status enum dropped -- including the specific combined kill-shot, status='closed' with no closure_evidence, wrongly validating; pattern anchor dropped, wrongly accepting a nested non-prefix path; top-level evidence minItems dropped; closure_evidence minItems-under-resolved dropped). Every mechanism confirmed sound in rounds 0-2 remains sound and was independently re-verified in this pass: the loose-vs-strict closure conditional (entries 13/18 remain load-bearing against a fresh LOOSE scratch variant), the 8-required-key absence loop, the confidence enum, the original wrong-prefix path-pattern entry, the minLength-on-five-fields entry, the maturity exact-vocabulary entry, the severity-not-an-enum design decision (unchanged design text, previously verbatim-confirmed against ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1), and the dual-surface NIZAM.json pair (re-verified against three fresh scratch variants built from the current, unchanged, post-037-commit real NIZAM.json -- still fully and uniquely distinguishing all three single-surface/dual cases). The fingerprint at HEAD 8c7c3ee was re-confirmed live and read-only across every git/NIZAM/README-dependent entry: results exactly match the design_notes-predicted pre-implementation state, unchanged in substance from the rev2 review. A sweep of the four new entries for fresh defect classes found none: no ungated echo, no cross-branch masking (entry 5's status probes are attributable solely to the enum, not the closure conditional), no shared-state leakage, and evidence_convention's stated count (35) matches the live array length exactly. One pre-existing, non-blocking observation (schema_design's 'id... unique within the audit run' prose claim, which is structurally outside this single-finding-object schema's own reach and unchanged since revision 0) is carried forward for the record only, not as a new finding, and does not gate approval. No further gap was found. Fit for implementation as-is.
